customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, high specificity. Larry is cooperative and available but has a non-standard ask (solar for a newly built, no-meter detached garage) and has stated he will shop competitors after this appointment. He is not hostile but he is transactional — he wants to know if the economics work for his specific setup before committing.
unresolved_concerns: Whether Ion can design and install solar for a no-meter detached garage (26x26, two-story, newly built this week)
Sizing and cost estimate for minimal solar covering power tools, lights, and outlets — Larry asked this directly and was told the specialist would answer it
Larry intends to call other companies for comparison quotes — appointment is at risk if competitors get to him first
sensitive_topics_raised: Larry wants solar only for the garage, not the home — do not pivot to home solar unless Larry opens that door
Garage has no separate meter — specialist must confirm Ion's capability on this configuration before the visit proceeds
exact_promises_rep_made: Specialist will visit in person tomorrow at 12:00 PM
Specialist will go over sizing and cost for the garage solar install
Rep will send a reconfirmation text a couple of hours before the visit
Rep noted Larry's sizing question in the appointment notes for the specialist
closer_should_know: Larry does not know the company name — he called it 'Thomann Solar' and the rep ambiguously confirmed it. The specialist should introduce ION Solar clearly at the door and not assume Larry has the right company in mind. Separately, the no-meter garage configuration is the first thing to confirm on-site — if Ion cannot serve this use case, the visit ends there and the rep's qualification was premature.
